title: buxus microphylla var. japonica
intro: Buxus microphylla var. japonica, also known as Japanese boxwood or boxwood, is a shrub species within the Buxaceae family. Traditionally, Buxus microphylla var. japonica has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as supporting cardiovascular health and improving digestion, although more research is required to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, Buxus microphylla var. japonica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to promote overall well-being and alleviate certain health issues.
